item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way I understood it, the Foundation's only goal here is to prevent more cell phones from manifesting. If she doesn't answer the phone, another one appears, so their containment procedures are dependent on making sure that she answers the phone by any means necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a bit confused about this line:

Designated personnel are to continue using aliased email addresses to send emails to Heather Hutchins indicating that she may receive an important call.

Why email her?

item.title: Awesome skip, and I love it. +1
item.content:encoded:
I like the skip.

Also, I've heard of helicopter parents and seen them and heck, even know them, so I can basically tell you that the pacing of the threats (Is the mom serious or not?) sounds exactly like that of a helicopter parent.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This induced physically nauseating anxiety in me. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot, but I'm really wondering how the logistics of a ghost controlling her daughter by threatening to withdraw financial support would work. Does ghost mom still somehow have control over her bank accounts? Is ghost mom also haunting her father and using him to control the money? Is Heather even aware that her mother is dead?Did she attend the funeral and get an inheritance? Is she just too afraid of ghost mom to call her out for making empty threats?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess I'll stick to novote, even though I actually like this piece more than I don't (if that makes any sense). The dialogues are done beautifully, and I did end up sympathizing with that poor girl. Luckily, I didn't have parents like this myself, but some of my friends did, so it did hit me with a pretty uncomfortable feeling.

However, I can't help but feel that the skip itself didn't do enough in terms of taking its own spin on this topic. The "horror" it presents lies in a pretty mundane thing, which is why it hits people hard, but aside from that, it doesn't really have much. I felt that the ending was pretty anticlimactic, and the anomaly itself utilizes a pretty standard ghost plot. I can see how for someone it might've been a simple yet interesting addition, because the story didn't necessarily need something more complex, but for me it felt like a pretty cliched way to handle this story.

TL;DR you've totally nailed the dialogue parts, but some others parts were, imo, unimpressive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …. So I see you've met my mother… lovely… +1 damn you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ambiguous* :P

Also, upvoted. I’m not the target audience, as my parents and the parents of my close friends have always been really chill — so, that stops me from getting the feels of this, but it doesn’t stop me from seeing that the characterisation is spot on, the pacing is good, writing is good, etc.. Keep it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know of 2190 but I didn't consider it when I was writing this. Namely because 2190 looks at this more from the perspective of when the victim has enough autonomy to take a stand on their own. The other thing I was trying to do with this that 2190 doesn't look into was the conversations themselves between Heather and -A. Exploring exactly how that power dynamic plays out between the two characters over the course of a conversation, because I think there's something very powerful in the ways that parents word certain responses to their kids. The condescension in particular is something I wanted to get at.

item.title: Besides being recent
item.content:encoded: It feels a bit too similar to 2190.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tentative +1

only tentative because I feel like the Foundation would want to contain an active anomaly—I'm not sure I agree with the in-article supposition that Ms. Hutchins isn't anomalous, the phones are being attracted to her at any rate.

On the flipside as others have pointed out this is pretty much exactly what having an overbearing parent is like. So you nailed that part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: there's something very ironic about her mother's death

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Indeed it should be! A staff member has addressed the issue.

Good metric for checking tags on the wiki: if there's only one or two pages that use a given tag, that tag isn't an official one used by the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: About the tag "ectroentropic" in this page.. I think it should be "ectoentropic"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! And regarding whether the threats were legitimate or not, that’s something I wanted to leave purposefully ambitious. In large part because Heather probably isn’t certain if they’re genuine, and I think that was an important part of the feeling I was trying to get across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, a lot of this felt too real. Some of my friends went through this sort of thing and it's chilling how close the dialogue is.

+1. Oof indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oof.

Loved this as a draft, and love it now. The anger I feel for Hutchins being treated so poorly by her ghost phone mother is particularly visceral, and I really wish I knew if SCP-4925-A's threats were legitimate or not.

+1 CK. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was enjoyable enough to upvote, but unfortunately had trouble shaking the feeling that SCP-2190 did this in a more interesting way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wonderful: excellent and upsetting. I liked it as a draft and I like it even better now! +1!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes me feel sick and uncomfortable. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This took my issues with phone calls from controlling parents and played them like a fiddle, leaving me more than slightly nauseated afterwards. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Likely because it was published about two hours ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Solid SCP to me. why is this so lowly rated?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not that the Foundation isn't doing any sort of containment here. They're trying to indirectly ensure that more cellphones don't manifest. They just decided that it wasn't worth their time to lock Heather up or something more drastic like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except they did. This article clearly says so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And I thought my parents were bad.

They are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this skip but the foundation would never ever deem containment unnecessary for an anomalous object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At some point Heather will grow sick of it and make bank selling an infinite supply of jail broken iphones

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The changes you've made since the draft phase helps this piece flow a lot better. It still hiccups here and there, but you've captured overbearing, obsessive, and abusive tenancies well within these characters. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-4925-A: Twenty minutes? Where did you go?

Hutchins: Chipotle?

SCP-4925-A: Why are you off campus when I bought you that meal plan? Do you want to be on your own for meals? Just say the word and I'll stop paying for it.

God this is fucking superb characterization of the stereotypical helicopter mom. Never an easier +1 in my life.

E: Seconding Niles in that this did honestly tie my stomach in knots in that way that only parental anxiety can.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I've heard people complain about ridiculous and absurd things that their parents have said or done to them a good amount. I wanted to take that sentiment and put it into a skip. I hope you enjoy this take on helicopter parenting.

Thanks to everyone who looked this over: Oboebandgeek99, ElogeeFishtruck, Tanhony, Conwell, Vezaz, aismallard, Henzoid, Rounderhouse, keyii, TRutherford, TheMightyMcB

Image credit: https://www.flickr.com/photos/osde-info/14473650674
